slide8.title: Product
slide8.subtitle: 3 parallel tracks · Q2 2026 → 2027+ · Infrastructure × Intelligence × Compliance

milestones.title: Core Shipping Engine
milestones.detail: Unified REST + GraphQL gateway aggregating 40+ carrier APIs (FedEx, DHL, UPS, SF Express, Cainiao). Automated label generation with barcode/QR, customs CN22/CN23, and return labels. Rate-shopping engine compares real-time quotes across all carriers.

milestones.title: Edge-Native Scaling
milestones.detail: Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects at 300+ PoPs worldwide. Guaranteed sub-100ms P95 latency for tracking and rate queries. Auto-scaling from 0 to 50K req/s with zero cold starts. Regional data residency for EU (Dublin), APAC (Tokyo), CN (Shanghai).

milestones.title: WMS Deep Sync
milestones.detail: Bi-directional inventory sync with ShipStation, ShipBob, Flexport, and custom WMS via webhooks + polling fallback. Real-time stock level mesh prevents overselling across 10+ sales channels. Pick-pack-ship workflow automation with barcode scan verification.

milestones.title: Global Expansion
milestones.detail: Full multi-tenant architecture supporting 500+ concurrent brand accounts. White-label portal with custom domains, branding, and email templates. Localized checkout with 25+ currencies and region-specific carrier preferences. Partner API marketplace for 3PL integrations.

milestones.title: AI Address Parsing
milestones.detail: Transformer-based NER model trained on 50M+ global addresses across 12 languages. Handles unstructured input, abbreviations, and transliteration (Chinese ↔ Pinyin, Japanese ↔ Romaji). 99.7% accuracy with auto-correction suggestions. Reduces failed deliveries by 35%.

milestones.title: MCP Protocol V1
milestones.detail: Model Context Protocol bridge connecting ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok to live logistics data. Natural language queries: 'Where is my shipment?' → real-time tracking + ETA. Tool-calling architecture with 12 registered functions: track, quote, book, cancel, reroute, documents, compliance, analytics, alerts, bulk, returns, customs.

milestones.title: IoT Node Network
milestones.detail: BLE/LoRa sensor mesh for temperature (-40°C to +80°C), humidity, shock, and light exposure. Real-time alerts via webhook + SMS + email when thresholds are breached. FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliant audit trail for pharmaceutical and food logistics. Dashboard with heatmaps showing cold-chain integrity across routes.

milestones.title: Autonomous Logistics
milestones.detail: Reinforcement learning engine that continuously optimizes carrier selection, routing, and consolidation. Predictive demand forecasting based on historical patterns + external signals (weather, holidays, strikes). Auto-rerouting around disruptions with cost-impact analysis. Target: 15% cost reduction and 22% faster delivery times.

milestones.title: GDPR & Privacy
milestones.detail: Full GDPR Article 28 compliance with dedicated EU data processing in Dublin. Supabase Row-Level Security (RLS) ensuring tenant data isolation. Automated data retention policies with right-to-erasure workflows. Privacy-by-design architecture with field-level encryption for PII.

milestones.title: Carbon ISO 14067
milestones.detail: ISO 14067-certified carbon footprint calculator for every shipment. Scope-3 emissions tracking across the full logistics chain: first-mile, line-haul, last-mile, warehousing. Carbon offset marketplace integration with verified credits (Gold Standard, Verra). Sustainability dashboard with monthly ESG reporting for enterprise clients.

milestones.title: Customs Autopilot
milestones.detail: AI-powered HS code classification with 98.5% accuracy across 5,000+ product categories. Automated generation of commercial invoices, packing lists, and certificates of origin. Direct integration with customs authorities in EU, US, CN, and JP for e-filing. Denied party screening and dual-use goods detection in real-time.

milestones.title: Green Standards Leader
milestones.detail: Carbon-neutral operations certified by Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi). Green carrier scoring algorithm prioritizing EVs, rail, and consolidated shipments. Packaging waste reduction program: AI-driven box-size optimization reduces void fill by 40%. Annual sustainability report with verified Scope 1-3 emissions data.

slide17.title: Carbon Credits
slide17.subtitle: Tradable instruments representing verified emission reductions — the bridge between corporate climate ambition and measurable impact.
slide17.tabHint: 👆 Click the tabs above to explore all sections

tabs.definition: A carbon credit is a tradable instrument representing one metric tonne of CO₂e (carbon dioxide equivalent) that is avoided (not emitted) or removed from the atmosphere and verified under a recognised standard. Organisations buy credits to address residual emissions after internal reduction efforts, or to meet obligations in regulated schemes.

concepts.title: Standardised Unit
concepts.body: 1 credit = 1 metric tonne of CO₂e. This standardisation allows different greenhouse gases (methane, nitrous oxide, etc.) to be compared on a common baseline.

concepts.title: Two Market Types
concepts.body: Compliance markets are regulated and obligation-driven (e.g., EU ETS). Voluntary markets are self-initiated to support net-zero claims and stakeholder expectations.

concepts.title: Avoidance vs Removal
concepts.body: Avoidance credits prevent emissions from occurring. Removal credits take CO₂ from the atmosphere. Removals are increasingly preferred for long-term net-zero strategies.

lifecycleSteps.label: Validate
lifecycleSteps.detail: A project's methodology and baseline are validated by an accredited third-party against a recognised standard (e.g., Verra VCS, Gold Standard).

lifecycleSteps.label: Verify
lifecycleSteps.detail: Emissions impacts are independently verified through monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) to confirm real, measurable reductions or removals.

lifecycleSteps.label: Issue
lifecycleSteps.detail: Verified credits are issued by a registry (Verra, Gold Standard, ACR) with unique serial numbers ensuring full traceability.

lifecycleSteps.label: Retire
lifecycleSteps.detail: Credits are permanently retired in the registry upon use, preventing double-counting and ensuring each tonne is claimed only once.

reasons.title: Strategy & License to Operate
reasons.body: Credits support credible transition plans by addressing hard-to-abate residual emissions while core decarbonisation investments scale up over time.

reasons.title: Financial Planning
reasons.body: In regulated markets, allowances/credits represent a real cost of carbon; in voluntary markets, they are a budget line that should be governed like any other external spend.

reasons.title: Risk Management
reasons.body: Poor-quality credits create reputational, legal, and audit risk — 'greenwashing' allegations can undermine ESG disclosures and destroy stakeholder trust.

reasons.title: Capital Mobilisation
reasons.body: High-integrity credit demand unlocks financing for projects (nature, energy, engineered removals) that may not proceed without carbon revenue.

reasons.title: Funding Gap Bridge
reasons.body: Revenue from credit sales often provides the necessary capital to make clean energy or conservation projects financially viable and bankable.

reasons.title: Corporate Targets
reasons.body: Companies like Microsoft and Tesla use carbon credits to address residual emissions as they work toward net-zero goals and SBTi-validated targets.

checklist.title: Additionality
checklist.body: Would the project happen without carbon revenue? The emission reduction must be beyond business-as-usual.

checklist.title: MRV Quality
checklist.body: Clear methodology, independent verification, transparent data — the backbone of credit integrity and auditability.

checklist.title: Permanence
checklist.body: For removals, how long does the carbon stay out of the atmosphere? Buffer pools and insurance mechanisms must exist.

checklist.title: No Leakage
checklist.body: Does the activity shift emissions elsewhere? For example, avoided deforestation must not simply displace logging to a nearby region.

checklist.title: No Double Counting
checklist.body: Confirm registry controls and claims alignment so the same tonne isn't claimed by multiple parties or under different schemes.

checklist.title: Retirement Evidence
checklist.body: Ensure credits are retired in the registry and documented for audit, disclosures, and regulatory submissions.

slide17.bottomNote: Aligned with ICVCM Core Carbon Principles · Verra VCS · Gold Standard · SBTi guidelines
